top_categories.workout_context: 62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: May in Cologne, Germany can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Jedermann-Rennen Rund um Köln’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Cologne, Germany.
